mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 55 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity utilization peaks that trigger short-term profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO surges during late-cycle phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the regime-specific predictive coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching 215 on the CPIAUCSL index forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or the threshold effect shows no incremental predictive power over a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a negative or null correlation with 5-day forward returns across a 5-year rolling window, or the signal is subsumed by monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts decline while retail sales rise, signals a sectoral rotation away from interest-sensitive assets that produces negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence signal predicts negative returns in fewer than 40 percent of triggered months, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Co-movement between industrial production and inflation during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply-side normalization that stabilizes input costs and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-CPIAUCSL signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 55 percent of post-shock recovery periods, or the regime-conditional coefficient loses significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.
